## 2. [Lesley Gao](https://www.linkedin.com/in/lesley-gao/)

_Product Designer | Web Developer_

**May 25, 2026** · Lesley worked with Chan on the same team

```
A PR merged at 12:30am? You didn't see it wrong, that's pretty normal for Chan.

I've really enjoyed working with Chan as a She Sharp ambassador and collaborating on the new website together. Since this is a project that requires commitment outside of work hours, it's not unusual to see PRs being merged late at night in the GitHub repo.

Chan is someone you feel genuinely confident working with. She naturally becomes the technical backbone of the team, and whenever technical support is needed, she's always one of the first to step up.

She is also proactive in proposing automation tools that improve efficiency and reduce costs for the team. Beyond that, she often goes out of her way to build things that genuinely help others learn. For example, during this year's MSD workshops, she independently created a website that brought together workshop resources and additional AI learning materials, giving participants more opportunities to continue exploring and learning beyond the sessions. To me, that is what true empowerment looks like.

From a communication perspective, Chan is very open to feedback. She acts quickly on reasonable suggestions, while also being confident enough to push back on ideas that may not make sense, always with thoughtful explanations or better alternatives.

Above all, what stands out most to me is her passion for technology. It's something I genuinely admire. I would wholeheartedly recommend Chan to any employer looking for a reliable, technically strong, and genuinely passionate developer.
```

## 3. [Saba Gecgil](https://www.linkedin.com/in/saba-gecgil/)

_Founder & CEO @ GAVIGO | Activation & Execution Layer for Interactive Software_

**May 25, 2026** · Saba managed Chan directly

```
Chan has been one of the most important engineering contributors to GAVIGO during a demanding early stage.

What stands out about Chan is not only his technical ability, but the way he turns ambiguous founder-level direction into working systems, measurable proof, and reliable product surfaces.

At GAVIGO, Chan has led critical work across our activation and execution pipeline, orchestration behavior, telemetry, demo reliability, public/investor/operator surfaces, and compute-backed proof systems. He consistently brings clarity to hard systems questions: what needs to run at activation time, what can be prepared, what can be reused, where state can be preserved, and where the boundary needs to be measured rather than assumed.

He combines speed with discipline. Chan ships quickly, but he also documents, tests, and pushes for technical honesty. If something is unsafe to expose, he says it clearly. If a fast fix is enough, he executes it. If a deeper system boundary appears, he helps turn it into a measured engineering question instead of hand-waving around it.

He has also been an excellent founder-engineer collaborator. In an early-stage company, priorities change, constraints are real, and the path is rarely linear. Chan has handled that environment with ownership, patience, and maturity. He cares not only about writing code, but about making the system demonstrable, explainable, and credible for investors, partners, and the team.

Chan played a major role in helping GAVIGO move from an early technical concept into a controlled proof system with real product behavior, measured infrastructure signals, and a much clearer activation/execution architecture.

I would strongly recommend Chan for any ambitious technical team building low-latency systems, activation infrastructure, orchestration, AI/compute-backed product surfaces, or early-stage deep technology. He is the kind of engineer who makes a hard system more real, more measurable, and more shippable.
```

**May 25, 2026** · Nirmala worked with Chan on the same team

```
I have had the pleasure of working alongside Chan Meng through her outstanding volunteer contributions at She Sharp, where she has taken a leading role in driving the organisation's website development initiative. Her dedication to advancing She Sharp's mission of bridging the gender gap in STEM is reflected not only in her technical expertise, but also in her commitment to creating meaningful impact for the community.
Chan Meng demonstrated exceptional leadership in spearheading the transition from a no-code Webflow platform to a modern full-stack development environment. She architected and developed the new She Sharp website using technologies such as Next.js 15, Tailwind CSS, PostgreSQL, and Drizzle ORM, while also implementing secure authentication systems and Stripe payment integration to support membership and event management. Her ability to combine technical innovation with practical, scalable solutions has significantly strengthened She Sharp's digital infrastructure and user experience.
Beyond her technical capabilities, Chan Meng is highly collaborative, detail-oriented, and proactive in her approach. She consistently brings thoughtful problem-solving, clear documentation practices, and a strong sense of ownership to every aspect of her work. Her contributions have helped create a scalable and professional platform that supports the She Sharp community.
I highly recommend Chan Meng for her technical excellence, leadership, and dedication to using technology to create positive social impact. She is a valuable contributor to any team, and I look forward to seeing her continued success in the tech industry.
```
